data.title: As a hook library maintainer, all inline copy-pasted ERR logging traps in hook libs and top-level hooks are consolidated into the shared handler
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Migrates ~24 inline `trap 'printf ...' ERR` logging traps across hook lib files and top-level hook scripts to use `_dso_hook_err_handler`. Removes all local `HOOK_ERROR_LOG="$HOME/.claude/hook-error-log.jsonl"` definitions (now centralised in the handler). Updates `run-hook.sh` to use the new log path.

**Why**: The inline traps are the primary source of duplication — each is a copy-paste with only the hook name changed. Migrating them to the shared handler eliminates ~24 repetitions and ensures all hooks automatically gain future handler improvements.

**Scope**:
- IN: 
  - `hooks/lib/pre-bash-functions.sh` (~4 inline traps: test-failure-guard, commit-failure-tracker, worktree-bash-guard, worktree-edit-guard)
  - `hooks/lib/pre-all-functions.sh` (1: checkpoint-rollback)
  - `hooks/lib/session-misc-functions.sh` (~3: plan-review-gate, brainstorm-gate, taskoutput-block-guard)
  - `hooks/lib/review-gate-bypass-sentinel.sh` (1 logging trap — enforcement `trap - ERR; return 2` patterns MUST be preserved unchanged)
  - `hooks/lib/post-functions.sh` (2 `trap 'return 0' ERR` patterns — see Considerations for special handling)
  - `hooks/lib/pre-edit-write-functions.sh` (any inline traps)
  - Top-level: `hooks/session-safety-check.sh`, `hooks/taskoutput-block-guard.sh`, `hooks/plan-review-gate.sh`, `hooks/track-cascade-failures.sh`, `hooks/title-length-validator.sh`, `hooks/track-tool-errors.sh`, `hooks/review-stop-check.sh`, `hooks/check-validation-failures.sh` (1 each)
  - `hooks/run-hook.sh` — updates local `HOOK_ERROR_LOG` path to `~/.claude/logs/dso-hook-errors.jsonl`
- OUT: Dispatcher files (S2/S3), enforcement hooks (S5), `trap - ERR` enforcement returns in review-gate-bypass-sentinel.sh

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, no file outside `hook-error-handler.sh` defines `HOOK_ERROR_LOG` as a local variable pointing to the legacy path `$HOME/.claude/hook-error-log.jsonl`
  ← Satisfies: "All ~24 existing copy-pasted logging traps migrated to shared handler"
- When this story is complete, all inline `trap 'printf ... >> HOOK_ERROR_LOG' ERR` patterns in the files listed in scope are replaced with `_dso_hook_err_handler` registration or explicit documented exceptions
  ← Satisfies: "All ~24 existing copy-pasted logging traps migrated to shared handler"
- When this story is complete, `hooks/run-hook.sh` writes to `~/.claude/logs/dso-hook-errors.jsonl`
  ← Satisfies: "Legacy path deprecated; all readers and test fixtures updated to new path"
- When this story is complete, all existing tests pass and unit tests are written and passing for all migrated functions

## Considerations

- [Correctness] `hooks/lib/post-functions.sh` uses `trap 'return 0' ERR` (function-scope exit, not process-scope). The shared handler must NOT change this to `exit 0` — doing so would cause the parent dispatcher to exit early instead of continuing past a failed sub-function. Migration options: (a) implement `_dso_hook_err_handler` with a mode parameter that uses `return 0` vs `exit 0`, or (b) leave `post-functions.sh` traps as `trap 'return 0' ERR` (no logging) and document the exception. Choose option that preserves caller behavior.
- [Correctness] `hooks/lib/review-gate-bypass-sentinel.sh` contains intentional `trap - ERR; return 2` patterns (enforcement returns, not logging traps). These MUST NOT be migrated — they are the enforcement mechanism. Only the one logging trap at function entry should be migrated.
- [Reliability] Lib functions are sourced into dispatcher contexts — `source hook-error-handler.sh` must not be called inside a function body (double-source risk). Source it once at the dispatcher level (S2/S3) and rely on it being available.
- [Testing] After migration, verify that the enforcement `trap - ERR; return 2` patterns in review-gate-bypass-sentinel.sh still fire correctly (these must block, not log-and-continue)

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
